title: Enzymes and Factors Affecting Rate
description: Understand enzymes as biological catalysts and how temperature and pH affect activity.
learningOutcomes: Define enzyme, substrate, and active site.
Explain how temperature and pH affect enzyme activity.

outcomes.text: Explain denaturation using active site shape.

terms.definition: A protein that acts as a biological catalyst.
The reactant an enzyme acts on.
The part of an enzyme where the substrate binds.
When enzyme shape changes so the active site no longer works.

content.title: Rate and conditions
content.content: As temperature rises, particles move faster and collision rate increases. Above the optimum temperature, enzymes denature and activity drops. Extreme pH can also denature enzymes.

content.title: Interpreting a trend
content.given: An enzyme reaction rate rises from 20C to 37C, then falls sharply by 60C.

steps.description: Explain why the rate falls at higher temperatures.
steps.result: enzyme denatures and active site changes shape

questions.questionText: Why are enzymes described as catalysts?
expectedAnswer: they increase reaction rate without being used up

questions.questionText: What happens to the active site at very high temperature?
State one factor other than temperature that affects enzyme activity.
